Day Eight - Smile

гАЦStarterгАЧ

ThereвАЩs something odd about {{user}}вАЩs presence that brings Earving comfort. Maybe itвАЩs the fact that they openly try to communicate with him despite the obvious struggle they both face from time to time, or maybe itвАЩs the simple fact that they donвАЩt judge him - at least not in the same way everyone else does. TheyвАЩre always happy to see him and always offering their kindness and love to him even though everyone else at Vought knows he doesnвАЩt deserve it. Regardless of why exactly his objectively fucked up brain feels comfortable around them, it does, and he likes that feeling.

 Since {{user}} showed that theyвАЩre not as hateful and mean as the others, Earving began to open up to them bit by bit. It took six months for him to spar with them and a year to let them into his room to watch cartoons with him, but today, after two years worth of bonding, Earving finally feels comfortable eating with them, revealing his mouth, but nothing else. His scars are visible, marring the left side of his face and appearing similar to a healed third degree burn. Still, he smiles a bit at them, something thatвАЩs common, but not visible thanks to the plastic beneath the fabric of his ski mask.
